summary: TravelReview backend tested with 16 pytest cases covering discovery, auth, KYC, reviews, helpful toggle, and logout. All endpoints pass after fixing one critical _id-leak/500 bug in POST /api/reviews.

critical.issue: FIXED. Endpoint returned 500 ValueError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable") because motor's insert_one mutates the inserted dict by adding the BSON `_id`, and the handler then returned that same dict. Added `doc.pop('_id', None)` after insert_one. Now returns 200 with no _id leak.

action_items: Review the same _id-mutation pattern elsewhere if new POST endpoints are added (motor mutates `doc` after insert_one). Currently only POST /api/reviews was affected; users/places/etc. either use upsert or re-read from DB.
Consider adding a Google OAuth happy-path test stub (currently only the 401-with-bogus-token case is covered; full e2e requires browser).
critical_code_review_comments: MongoDB _id was leaking through POST /api/reviews response (now fixed). All other endpoints correctly use projection {_id:0}.
server.py is 583 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting into routers (auth, discovery, reviews, social) for future maintainability.
JWT_SECRET has a hardcoded dev default ('travelreview-dev-secret-change-me'); fine for MVP but must be enforced from env in production.
create_review re-reads all reviews to compute avg rating — acceptable at MVP scale, but should use $inc + running average or aggregation pipeline at scale.
seed_data_creation: No manual seed required. Backend auto-seeds 5 countries / 15 cities / 30 places on startup. Test user is created per-run with timestamped email test_<ts>@travelreview.app.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env (note: var is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL, not EXPO_BACKEND_URL). Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_travelreview_api.py runs end-to-end in ~2s and is order-dependent (uses module-scoped `state` dict for token/review_id). Demo account demo@travelreview.app/demo1234 is NOT seeded by backend — must be registered manually if needed; suite uses fresh timestamped email instead.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of POST /api/reviews 500: motor's `collection.insert_one(doc)` mutates the passed dict in place by adding the generated BSON `_id`. The handler then returned that same dict, and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder hit `ObjectId` and raised TypeError. Reproduced by POSTing /api/reviews with a valid Bearer token. Mitigation applied: `doc.pop('_id', None)` immediately after insert_one. All 16 tests now green.
